title: buildah run doesn't parse commands with "dash" options without a tty
body:
It looks like `buildah run` is not correctly parsing command arguments with a dash in them.  Arguments are handled correctly if there are no dashes.

Example (from the docs: https://github.com/projectatomic/buildah/blob/master/docs/buildah-run.md):

```
# container=$(buildah from centos:centos7)
buildah run $container 'ps -auxw'
container_linux.go:259: starting container process caused "exec: \"ps -auxw\": executable file not found in $PATH"
```
Other examples:

```
# buildah run $container ls /
anaconda-post.log  dev	home  lib64	  media  opt   root  sbin  sys	usr
bin		   etc	lib   lost+found  mnt	 proc  run   srv   tmp	var

# buildah run $container ls -la /
Incorrect Usage: flag provided but not defined: -la

NAME:
   buildah run - Run a command inside of the container

...<snip>...
   
ERRO[0000] flag provided but not defined: -la 
```

Escaping or quoting doesn't help:

```
# buildah run $container ls \-la /
Incorrect Usage: flag provided but not defined: -la

NAME:
   buildah run - Run a command inside of the container

...<snip>...
  
   
ERRO[0000] flag provided but not defined: -la  
```

However it works if the --tty flag is set:
```
# buildah run --tty $container ls -la /
total 20
drwxr-xr-x.   1 root root    40 Jul 17 20:12 .
drwxr-xr-x.   1 root root    40 Jul 17 20:12 ..
-rw-r--r--.   1 root root 15759 Jul  5 17:16 anaconda-post.log
lrwxrwxrwx.   1 root root     7 Jul  5 17:14 bin -> usr/bin
drwxr-xr-x.   5 root root   360 Jul 17 20:16 dev
drwxr-xr-x.  47 root root  4096 Jul  5 17:16 etc
drwxr-xr-x.   2 root root     6 Nov  5  2016 home
lrwxrwxrwx.   1 root root     7 Jul  5 17:14 lib -> usr/lib
lrwxrwxrwx.   1 root root     9 Jul  5 17:14 lib64 -> usr/lib64
drwx------.   2 root root     6 Jul  5 17:14 lost+found
drwxr-xr-x.   2 root root     6 Nov  5  2016 media
drwxr-xr-x.   2 root root     6 Nov  5  2016 mnt
drwxr-xr-x.   2 root root     6 Nov  5  2016 opt
dr-xr-xr-x. 326 root root     0 Jul 17 20:16 proc
dr-xr-x---.   2 root root   137 Jul  5 17:16 root
drwxr-xr-x.  10 root root   130 Jul  5 17:16 run
lrwxrwxrwx.   1 root root     8 Jul  5 17:14 sbin -> usr/sbin
drwxr-xr-x.   2 root root     6 Nov  5  2016 srv
dr-xr-xr-x.  13 root root     0 Jul 12 13:02 sys
drwxrwxrwt.   7 root root   132 Jul  5 17:16 tmp
drwxr-xr-x.  13 root root   155 Jul  5 17:14 usr
drwxr-xr-x.  18 root root   238 Jul  5 17:16 var
```

It also works if there are no dashes in the command, tty or no:

```
buildah run --tty $container ps aux
USER       PID %CPU %MEM    VSZ   RSS TTY      STAT START   TIME COMMAND
root         1  0.0  0.0  45316  2964 pts/0    Rs+  20:20   0:00 ps aux
```

Buildah version:
```
# buildah -v
buildah version 0.1 (image-spec 1.0.0-rc6-dev, runtime-spec 1.0.0-rc5)
```

comments.body: Since the patch is merged closing this issue.  I have built new packages for Fedora.
